text: So our second use for our shortbread dough is going to be alfajores, one of my all-time favourites.
It's an Argentinian cookie that's sandwiched together with dulce de leche and then rolled in coconut.
It's absolutely delicious.
All we've done is taken our same basic dough, just replacing half of the flour with corn flour,
and all that does is it gives the biscuits a really nice melt-in-the-mouth texture.
So all we're going to do is take half of our dough at a time,
place it between two sheets of parchment paper, and just roll it out until it's about four or five millimetres thick.
So once our dough is rolled out, we're just going to slide that onto a baking tray
and then pop both those into the fridge for about an hour or so, just until really firm,
and then we can cut out our cookies and bake them.
So once our dough is nice and chilled, we can peel off our top sheet of parchment
and then place that onto our baking tray.
And then using a plain five centimetre round cookie cutter,
just cut out as many discs of the dough as you can and place those onto your baking tray.
And just leave a couple of centimetres between.
They won't spread a huge amount, but you don't want them to stick together.
Don't throw away the rest of the scraps.
You can re-roll those and just chill them down again before cutting out more cookies
so you use up all the dough and don't waste anything.
These are going to go into a slightly lower temperature, about 160 or 140 if you're using a fan oven,
for about 20 minutes, just until they're set and very lightly browned around the outside.
So these are our finished cookies.
We've just let them cool before we fill them with the rich dulce de leche.
It's just a really nice caramel.
It's sometimes sold as milk caramel,
carnation caramel, or dulce de leche.
So all we do is take one of our cookies,
and then spoon on a teaspoon or so of the caramel,
and then take a second biscuit and just press on top,
just until the caramel starts to peek out the outside.
And then roll it into our coconut,
and just repeat that with all the cookies until you're done.
Whilst they're really delicious now, as they are,
what I like to do is just let them sit for a couple of hours because the biscuit starts to soften,
and you get this really nice soft crumbly finish which for me is just delicious.
Now you don't have to bake them all off at the same time, you can bake off one or two
and pop the rest back in the fridge.
